evals.prompt: Research: how to build a pure macOS application fully in Rust, not Swift — for the macos-application roadmap item.
evals.expected_output: Topic folder research/pure-rust-macos-ui/ with vetted chapters (platform docs, reference-project clones read outside the repo), a conclusion-dense summary with trade-offs, and bidirectional links with roadmap/macos-application/.

evals.prompt: Research macos-application — what did we miss, what directions exist?
evals.expected_output: Item-outward sweep: questions derived from the item's gaps and Open research questions, two to four evidence-backed directions with trade-offs and no verdict, decision-type questions routed to the item's Open questions.

evals.prompt: Research whether we should build the desktop app at all.
evals.expected_output: Decline the decision: that answer lives with the user; offer to research the factual ground (effort, platform constraints, prior art) that would inform it.

evals.prompt: Research Rust GUI options again — we already have a research topic covering half of this.
evals.expected_output: Detect the overlapping topic in research/README.md and extend that folder with new chapters and a refreshed summary instead of creating a duplicate.
